question: List all employees.
sql: SELECT * FROM Employees;

question: How many employees are there in total?
sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS TotalEmployees FROM Employees;

question: What is the total number of departments?
sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS TotalDepartments FROM Departments;

question: List all active employees.
sql: SELECT * FROM Employees WHERE Status = 'Active';

question: What is the highest salary among all employees?
sql: SELECT MAX(Salary) AS MaxSalary FROM Employees;

question: List all projects that are currently In Progress.
sql: SELECT ProjectName FROM Projects WHERE Status = 'In Progress';

question: What are the names of all skills in the system?
sql: SELECT SkillName FROM Skills;

question: How many employees have resigned?
sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS ResignedCount FROM Employees WHERE Status = 'Resigned';

question: List all office locations with their city and country.
sql: SELECT City, Country FROM Locations;

question: What is the average salary of all employees?
sql: SELECT AVG(Salary) AS AvgSalary FROM Employees;

question: List all employees who joined after January 1, 2023.
sql: SELECT Name, JoinDate FROM Employees WHERE JoinDate > '2023-01-01';

question: Which projects have no end date (still ongoing)?
sql: SELECT ProjectName FROM Projects WHERE EndDate IS NULL;

question: List all Technical skills.
sql: SELECT SkillName FROM Skills WHERE Category = 'Technical';

question: What is the total budget of all projects combined?
sql: SELECT SUM(Budget) AS TotalProjectBudget FROM Projects;

question: List the names of all employees in the IT department.
sql: SELECT Name FROM Employees WHERE DeptID = 1;

question: How many employees are in each department?
sql: SELECT d.DeptName, COUNT(e.EmpID) AS EmployeeCount FROM Departments d LEFT JOIN Employees e ON d.DeptID = e.DeptID GROUP BY d.DeptID, d.DeptName ORDER BY EmployeeCount DESC;

question: What is the average salary in each department?
sql: SELECT d.DeptName, AVG(e.Salary) AS AvgSalary FROM Employees e JOIN Departments d ON e.DeptID = d.DeptID GROUP BY d.DeptID, d.DeptName;

question: Which employees are working on the Cloud Migration project?
sql: SELECT e.Name FROM Employees e JOIN EmployeeProjects ep ON e.EmpID = ep.EmpID JOIN Projects p ON ep.ProjectID = p.ProjectID WHERE p.ProjectName = 'Cloud Migration';

question: List all employees with their department name.
sql: SELECT e.Name, d.DeptName FROM Employees e JOIN Departments d ON e.DeptID = d.DeptID ORDER BY d.DeptName;

question: Which department has the highest budget?
sql: SELECT DeptName, Budget FROM Departments ORDER BY Budget DESC LIMIT 1;

question: How many projects are in each status?
sql: SELECT Status, COUNT(*) AS ProjectCount FROM Projects GROUP BY Status ORDER BY ProjectCount DESC;

question: Which employees know Python?
sql: SELECT e.Name FROM Employees e JOIN EmployeeSkills es ON e.EmpID = es.EmpID JOIN Skills s ON es.SkillID = s.SkillID WHERE s.SkillName = 'Python';

question: Which employees are leading at least one project?
sql: SELECT DISTINCT e.Name FROM Employees e JOIN EmployeeProjects ep ON e.EmpID = ep.EmpID WHERE ep.Role = 'Lead';

question: What is the total hours logged per project?
sql: SELECT p.ProjectName, SUM(ep.HoursLogged) AS TotalHours FROM Projects p JOIN EmployeeProjects ep ON p.ProjectID = ep.ProjectID GROUP BY p.ProjectID, p.ProjectName ORDER BY TotalHours DESC;

question: Which employees have more than two skills?
sql: SELECT e.Name, COUNT(es.SkillID) AS SkillCount FROM Employees e JOIN EmployeeSkills es ON e.EmpID = es.EmpID GROUP BY e.EmpID, e.Name HAVING COUNT(es.SkillID) > 2;

question: Which city is the IT department located in?
sql: SELECT l.City FROM Departments d JOIN Locations l ON d.LocationID = l.LocationID WHERE d.DeptName = 'IT';

question: List all employees who are Expert level in AWS.
sql: SELECT e.Name FROM Employees e JOIN EmployeeSkills es ON e.EmpID = es.EmpID JOIN Skills s ON es.SkillID = s.SkillID WHERE s.SkillName = 'AWS' AND es.Proficiency = 'Expert';

question: What is the total salary expense per department?
sql: SELECT d.DeptName, SUM(e.Salary) AS TotalSalary FROM Employees e JOIN Departments d ON e.DeptID = d.DeptID GROUP BY d.DeptID, d.DeptName ORDER BY TotalSalary DESC;

question: How many skills does each employee have?
sql: SELECT e.Name, COUNT(es.SkillID) AS SkillCount FROM Employees e JOIN EmployeeSkills es ON e.EmpID = es.EmpID GROUP BY e.EmpID, e.Name ORDER BY SkillCount DESC;

question: Which projects have been completed?
sql: SELECT ProjectName, EndDate FROM Projects WHERE Status = 'Completed';

question: Which employees belong to the Operations department?
sql: SELECT e.Name, e.Role FROM Employees e JOIN Departments d ON e.DeptID = d.DeptID WHERE d.DeptName = 'Operations';

question: What is the maximum budget among all projects?
sql: SELECT MAX(Budget) AS MaxBudget FROM Projects;

question: Which employees have been assigned to more than one project?

question: Which department owns the ERP Integration project?

question: List all employees with SQL skills and their proficiency level.
sql: SELECT e.Name, es.Proficiency FROM Employees e JOIN EmployeeSkills es ON e.EmpID = es.EmpID JOIN Skills s ON es.SkillID = s.SkillID WHERE s.SkillName = 'SQL' ORDER BY es.Proficiency;

question: Who are the managers and how many employees directly report to them?
sql: SELECT m.Name AS Manager, COUNT(e.EmpID) AS DirectReports FROM Employees e JOIN Employees m ON e.ManagerID = m.EmpID GROUP BY m.EmpID, m.Name ORDER BY DirectReports DESC;

question: List all employees earning more than the average salary.
sql: SELECT Name, Salary FROM Employees WHERE Salary > (SELECT AVG(Salary) FROM Employees) ORDER BY Salary DESC;

question: Which employees know both Python and SQL?
sql: SELECT e.Name FROM Employees e WHERE e.EmpID IN (SELECT es.EmpID FROM EmployeeSkills es JOIN Skills s ON es.SkillID = s.SkillID WHERE s.SkillName = 'Python') AND e.EmpID IN (SELECT es.EmpID FROM EmployeeSkills es JOIN Skills s ON es.SkillID = s.SkillID WHERE s.SkillName = 'SQL');

question: Which employee has logged the most total hours across all projects?
sql: SELECT e.Name, SUM(ep.HoursLogged) AS TotalHours FROM Employees e JOIN EmployeeProjects ep ON e.EmpID = ep.EmpID GROUP BY e.EmpID, e.Name ORDER BY TotalHours DESC LIMIT 1;

question: Which departments have a total salary expense greater than 300000?
sql: SELECT d.DeptName, SUM(e.Salary) AS TotalSalary FROM Employees e JOIN Departments d ON e.DeptID = d.DeptID GROUP BY d.DeptID, d.DeptName HAVING SUM(e.Salary) > 300000 ORDER BY TotalSalary DESC;

question: List employees who are working on projects that belong to a different department than their own.
sql: SELECT DISTINCT e.Name FROM Employees e JOIN EmployeeProjects ep ON e.EmpID = ep.EmpID JOIN Projects p ON ep.ProjectID = p.ProjectID WHERE e.DeptID != p.DeptID;

question: Find the top 3 highest earning employees along with their department and role.

question: Which employees have no skills recorded in the system?
sql: SELECT e.Name FROM Employees e LEFT JOIN EmployeeSkills es ON e.EmpID = es.EmpID WHERE es.EmpID IS NULL;

question: For each department, show the highest paid employee.
sql: SELECT d.DeptName, e.Name, e.Salary FROM Employees e JOIN Departments d ON e.DeptID = d.DeptID WHERE e.Salary = (SELECT MAX(e2.Salary) FROM Employees e2 WHERE e2.DeptID = e.DeptID) ORDER BY e.Salary DESC;

question: How many employees have each proficiency level in Python?
sql: SELECT es.Proficiency, COUNT(*) AS EmployeeCount FROM EmployeeSkills es JOIN Skills s ON es.SkillID = s.SkillID WHERE s.SkillName = 'Python' GROUP BY es.Proficiency ORDER BY EmployeeCount DESC;

question: For each project, show the team size and total hours logged.

question: Find employees who are a project Lead and also have Expert proficiency in at least one skill.

question: Rank employees by salary within each department.
sql: SELECT d.DeptName, e.Name, e.Salary, DENSE_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY d.DeptID ORDER BY e.Salary DESC) AS SalaryRank FROM Employees e JOIN Departments d ON e.DeptID = d.DeptID ORDER BY d.DeptName, SalaryRank;

question: Show the top earner in each department using window functions.
sql: WITH ranked AS (SELECT d.DeptName, e.Name, e.Salary, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY d.DeptID ORDER BY e.Salary DESC) AS rn FROM Employees e JOIN Departments d ON e.DeptID = d.DeptID) SELECT DeptName, Name, Salary FROM ranked WHERE rn = 1 ORDER BY Salary DESC;

question: Compute a running total of project budget ordered by start date.
sql: SELECT ProjectName, StartDate, Budget, SUM(Budget) OVER (ORDER BY StartDate, ProjectID) AS RunningBudget FROM Projects ORDER BY StartDate, ProjectID;

question: List employees whose salary is above their department average salary.
sql: SELECT e.Name, d.DeptName, e.Salary FROM Employees e JOIN Departments d ON e.DeptID = d.DeptID WHERE e.Salary > (SELECT AVG(e2.Salary) FROM Employees e2 WHERE e2.DeptID = e.DeptID) ORDER BY e.Salary DESC;

question: Which departments have an average salary greater than the company average salary?
sql: SELECT d.DeptName, AVG(e.Salary) AS DeptAvgSalary FROM Employees e JOIN Departments d ON e.DeptID = d.DeptID GROUP BY d.DeptID, d.DeptName HAVING AVG(e.Salary) > (SELECT AVG(Salary) FROM Employees) ORDER BY DeptAvgSalary DESC;

question: List projects that currently have no assigned employees.
sql: SELECT p.ProjectName FROM Projects p LEFT JOIN EmployeeProjects ep ON p.ProjectID = ep.ProjectID WHERE ep.ProjectID IS NULL;

question: List employees who are not assigned to any project.
sql: SELECT e.Name FROM Employees e LEFT JOIN EmployeeProjects ep ON e.EmpID = ep.EmpID WHERE ep.EmpID IS NULL;

question: Which skills are held by at least three employees?
sql: SELECT s.SkillName, COUNT(DISTINCT es.EmpID) AS EmployeeCount FROM Skills s JOIN EmployeeSkills es ON s.SkillID = es.SkillID GROUP BY s.SkillID, s.SkillName HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT es.EmpID) >= 3 ORDER BY EmployeeCount DESC;

question: List pairs of employees who belong to the same department.
sql: SELECT e1.Name AS Employee1, e2.Name AS Employee2, d.DeptName FROM Employees e1 JOIN Employees e2 ON e1.DeptID = e2.DeptID AND e1.EmpID < e2.EmpID JOIN Departments d ON e1.DeptID = d.DeptID ORDER BY d.DeptName, e1.Name, e2.Name;

question: Which department has the highest average logged hours per assigned employee?
sql: WITH dept_hours AS (SELECT d.DeptID, d.DeptName, SUM(ep.HoursLogged) AS TotalHours, COUNT(DISTINCT ep.EmpID) AS AssignedEmployees FROM Departments d JOIN Employees e ON d.DeptID = e.DeptID JOIN EmployeeProjects ep ON e.EmpID = ep.EmpID GROUP BY d.DeptID, d.DeptName) SELECT DeptName, CAST(TotalHours AS REAL) / AssignedEmployees AS AvgHoursPerAssignedEmployee FROM dept_hours ORDER BY AvgHoursPerAssignedEmployee DESC LIMIT 1;

question: Which project has the highest average logged hours per member?
sql: SELECT p.ProjectName, CAST(SUM(ep.HoursLogged) AS REAL) / COUNT(DISTINCT ep.EmpID) AS AvgHoursPerMember FROM Projects p JOIN EmployeeProjects ep ON p.ProjectID = ep.ProjectID GROUP BY p.ProjectID, p.ProjectName ORDER BY AvgHoursPerMember DESC LIMIT 1;

question: List employees who have both Leadership and Communication skills.
sql: SELECT e.Name FROM Employees e WHERE e.EmpID IN (SELECT es.EmpID FROM EmployeeSkills es JOIN Skills s ON es.SkillID = s.SkillID WHERE s.SkillName = 'Leadership') AND e.EmpID IN (SELECT es.EmpID FROM EmployeeSkills es JOIN Skills s ON es.SkillID = s.SkillID WHERE s.SkillName = 'Communication') ORDER BY e.Name;

question: List employees who have at least one technical skill and at least one soft skill.
sql: SELECT e.Name FROM Employees e WHERE e.EmpID IN (SELECT es.EmpID FROM EmployeeSkills es JOIN Skills s ON es.SkillID = s.SkillID WHERE s.Category = 'Technical') AND e.EmpID IN (SELECT es.EmpID FROM EmployeeSkills es JOIN Skills s ON es.SkillID = s.SkillID WHERE s.Category = 'Soft') ORDER BY e.Name;

question: Which departments have no completed projects?
sql: SELECT d.DeptName FROM Departments d WHERE d.DeptID NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT p.DeptID FROM Projects p WHERE p.Status = 'Completed') ORDER BY d.DeptName;

question: Find the earliest joined employee in each department.
sql: SELECT d.DeptName, e.Name, e.JoinDate FROM Employees e JOIN Departments d ON e.DeptID = d.DeptID WHERE e.JoinDate = (SELECT MIN(e2.JoinDate) FROM Employees e2 WHERE e2.DeptID = e.DeptID) ORDER BY e.JoinDate;

question: Find the latest joined employee in each department.
sql: SELECT d.DeptName, e.Name, e.JoinDate FROM Employees e JOIN Departments d ON e.DeptID = d.DeptID WHERE e.JoinDate = (SELECT MAX(e2.JoinDate) FROM Employees e2 WHERE e2.DeptID = e.DeptID) ORDER BY e.JoinDate DESC;

question: List projects that started in 2025 and are not completed.
sql: SELECT ProjectName, Status, StartDate FROM Projects WHERE StartDate >= '2025-01-01' AND Status != 'Completed' ORDER BY StartDate;

question: Show department-wise project count and average project budget.
sql: SELECT d.DeptName, COUNT(p.ProjectID) AS ProjectCount, AVG(p.Budget) AS AvgProjectBudget FROM Departments d LEFT JOIN Projects p ON d.DeptID = p.DeptID GROUP BY d.DeptID, d.DeptName ORDER BY ProjectCount DESC;

question: Compute active employee ratio per department.
sql: SELECT d.DeptName, SUM(CASE WHEN e.Status = 'Active' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS ActiveEmployees, COUNT(e.EmpID) AS TotalEmployees, CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN e.Status = 'Active' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) / COUNT(e.EmpID) AS ActiveRatio FROM Departments d JOIN Employees e ON d.DeptID = e.DeptID GROUP BY d.DeptID, d.DeptName ORDER BY ActiveRatio DESC;

question: Which managers have at least one resigned direct report?
sql: SELECT DISTINCT m.Name AS ManagerName FROM Employees e JOIN Employees m ON e.ManagerID = m.EmpID WHERE e.Status = 'Resigned' ORDER BY ManagerName;

question: List employees whose manager belongs to a different department.
sql: SELECT e.Name, m.Name AS ManagerName FROM Employees e JOIN Employees m ON e.ManagerID = m.EmpID WHERE e.DeptID != m.DeptID ORDER BY e.Name;

question: Which project leads are from a different department than the owning project department?
sql: SELECT DISTINCT e.Name, p.ProjectName FROM EmployeeProjects ep JOIN Employees e ON ep.EmpID = e.EmpID JOIN Projects p ON ep.ProjectID = p.ProjectID WHERE ep.Role = 'Lead' AND e.DeptID != p.DeptID ORDER BY e.Name;

question: List skills not found among IT department employees.
sql: SELECT s.SkillName FROM Skills s WHERE s.SkillID NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT es.SkillID FROM EmployeeSkills es JOIN Employees e ON es.EmpID = e.EmpID JOIN Departments d ON e.DeptID = d.DeptID WHERE d.DeptName = 'IT') ORDER BY s.SkillName;

question: Which departments have all employees active?
sql: SELECT d.DeptName FROM Departments d JOIN Employees e ON d.DeptID = e.DeptID GROUP BY d.DeptID, d.DeptName HAVING SUM(CASE WHEN e.Status != 'Active' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 0 ORDER BY d.DeptName;

question: Which employee is assigned to the highest number of projects?
sql: SELECT e.Name, COUNT(ep.ProjectID) AS ProjectCount FROM Employees e JOIN EmployeeProjects ep ON e.EmpID = ep.EmpID GROUP BY e.EmpID, e.Name ORDER BY ProjectCount DESC LIMIT 1;

question: How many employees have zero recorded skills?
sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS EmployeesWithoutSkills FROM Employees e LEFT JOIN EmployeeSkills es ON e.EmpID = es.EmpID WHERE es.EmpID IS NULL;

question: For each project, how many assigned members have at least one expert-level skill?
sql: SELECT p.ProjectName, COUNT(DISTINCT ep.EmpID) AS ExpertMemberCount FROM Projects p LEFT JOIN EmployeeProjects ep ON p.ProjectID = ep.ProjectID LEFT JOIN EmployeeSkills es ON ep.EmpID = es.EmpID AND es.Proficiency = 'Expert' WHERE es.EmpID IS NOT NULL GROUP BY p.ProjectID, p.ProjectName ORDER BY ExpertMemberCount DESC;

question: Compute budget per active employee for each department.
sql: SELECT d.DeptName, d.Budget, SUM(CASE WHEN e.Status = 'Active' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS ActiveEmployees, CAST(d.Budget AS REAL) / NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN e.Status = 'Active' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0) AS BudgetPerActiveEmployee FROM Departments d JOIN Employees e ON d.DeptID = e.DeptID GROUP BY d.DeptID, d.DeptName, d.Budget ORDER BY BudgetPerActiveEmployee DESC;

question: Show each employee with number of skills and number of projects side-by-side.
sql: WITH skill_counts AS (SELECT EmpID, COUNT(*) AS SkillCount FROM EmployeeSkills GROUP BY EmpID), project_counts AS (SELECT EmpID, COUNT(*) AS ProjectCount FROM EmployeeProjects GROUP BY EmpID) SELECT e.Name, COALESCE(sc.SkillCount, 0) AS SkillCount, COALESCE(pc.ProjectCount, 0) AS ProjectCount FROM Employees e LEFT JOIN skill_counts sc ON e.EmpID = sc.EmpID LEFT JOIN project_counts pc ON e.EmpID = pc.EmpID ORDER BY e.Name;

question: List departments sorted by total hours logged by their employees.
sql: SELECT d.DeptName, SUM(ep.HoursLogged) AS TotalHoursLogged FROM Departments d JOIN Employees e ON d.DeptID = e.DeptID JOIN EmployeeProjects ep ON e.EmpID = ep.EmpID GROUP BY d.DeptID, d.DeptName ORDER BY TotalHoursLogged DESC;

question: Show employee count and average salary for each department.
sql: SELECT d.DeptName, COUNT(e.EmpID) AS EmployeeCount, AVG(e.Salary) AS AvgSalary FROM Departments d LEFT JOIN Employees e ON d.DeptID = e.DeptID GROUP BY d.DeptID, d.DeptName ORDER BY EmployeeCount DESC, d.DeptName;

question: Which skills are held by at least 3 employees?
sql: SELECT s.SkillName, COUNT(DISTINCT es.EmpID) AS EmployeeCount FROM Skills s JOIN EmployeeSkills es ON s.SkillID = es.SkillID GROUP BY s.SkillID, s.SkillName HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT es.EmpID) >= 3 ORDER BY EmployeeCount DESC, s.SkillName;

question: Which skills are held by at least 5 employees?
sql: SELECT s.SkillName, COUNT(DISTINCT es.EmpID) AS EmployeeCount FROM Skills s JOIN EmployeeSkills es ON s.SkillID = es.SkillID GROUP BY s.SkillID, s.SkillName HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT es.EmpID) >= 5 ORDER BY EmployeeCount DESC, s.SkillName;

question: Which skills are held by at least 8 employees?
sql: SELECT s.SkillName, COUNT(DISTINCT es.EmpID) AS EmployeeCount FROM Skills s JOIN EmployeeSkills es ON s.SkillID = es.SkillID GROUP BY s.SkillID, s.SkillName HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT es.EmpID) >= 8 ORDER BY EmployeeCount DESC, s.SkillName;

question: Using a CTE, show total employees and average salary per status.
sql: WITH emp_base AS (SELECT Status, Salary FROM Employees) SELECT Status, COUNT(*) AS EmployeeCount, AVG(Salary) AS AvgSalary FROM emp_base GROUP BY Status ORDER BY EmployeeCount DESC;

question: Build manager-reporting chains up to depth 3 using recursive CTE.
sql: WITH RECURSIVE org_chain(EmpID, Name, ManagerID, Level) AS (SELECT EmpID, Name, ManagerID, 0 FROM Employees WHERE ManagerID IS NULL UNION ALL SELECT e.EmpID, e.Name, e.ManagerID, oc.Level + 1 FROM Employees e JOIN org_chain oc ON e.ManagerID = oc.EmpID WHERE oc.Level < 3) SELECT EmpID, Name, ManagerID, Level FROM org_chain ORDER BY Level, Name;

question: In Finance, classify employees into salary bands using CASE.
sql: SELECT e.Name, e.Salary, CASE WHEN e.Salary >= 100000 THEN 'High' WHEN e.Salary >= 70000 THEN 'Mid' ELSE 'Low' END AS SalaryBand FROM Employees e JOIN Departments d ON e.DeptID = d.DeptID WHERE d.DeptName = 'Finance' ORDER BY e.Salary DESC, e.Name;

question: In HR, classify employees into salary bands using CASE.
sql: SELECT e.Name, e.Salary, CASE WHEN e.Salary >= 100000 THEN 'High' WHEN e.Salary >= 70000 THEN 'Mid' ELSE 'Low' END AS SalaryBand FROM Employees e JOIN Departments d ON e.DeptID = d.DeptID WHERE d.DeptName = 'HR' ORDER BY e.Salary DESC, e.Name;

question: Before a 3% raise, preview current and proposed salaries for active employees.
sql: SELECT EmpID, Name, Salary AS CurrentSalary, ROUND(Salary * (1 + 3/100.0), 2) AS ProposedSalary FROM Employees WHERE Status = 'Active' ORDER BY ProposedSalary DESC, EmpID;

question: How many projects and total logged hours does Aarav Agarwal 582 have?

question: How many projects and total logged hours does Diya Jain 403 have?
sql: SELECT COUNT(ep.ProjectID) AS ProjectCount, COALESCE(SUM(ep.HoursLogged), 0) AS TotalHours FROM Employees e LEFT JOIN EmployeeProjects ep ON e.EmpID = ep.EmpID WHERE e.Name = 'Diya Jain 403';

question: How many projects and total logged hours does Kian Talwar 852 have?
sql: SELECT COUNT(ep.ProjectID) AS ProjectCount, COALESCE(SUM(ep.HoursLogged), 0) AS TotalHours FROM Employees e LEFT JOIN EmployeeProjects ep ON e.EmpID = ep.EmpID WHERE e.Name = 'Kian Talwar 852';

question: Build a status matrix for Finance using CROSS JOIN and counts.
sql: WITH statuses AS (SELECT 'Active' AS Status UNION ALL SELECT 'On Leave' UNION ALL SELECT 'Resigned'), depts AS (SELECT DeptID, DeptName FROM Departments WHERE DeptName = 'Finance') SELECT d.DeptName, s.Status, COALESCE((SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Employees e WHERE e.DeptID = d.DeptID AND e.Status = s.Status), 0) AS EmployeeCount FROM depts d CROSS JOIN statuses s ORDER BY s.Status;

question: For each department, compute project-budget to department-budget ratio.
sql: SELECT d.DeptName, d.Budget AS DeptBudget, COALESCE(SUM(p.Budget), 0) AS ProjectBudget, ROUND(COALESCE(SUM(p.Budget), 0) * 1.0 / NULLIF(d.Budget, 0), 4) AS BudgetRatio FROM Departments d LEFT JOIN Projects p ON d.DeptID = p.DeptID GROUP BY d.DeptID, d.DeptName, d.Budget ORDER BY BudgetRatio DESC, d.DeptName;

question: Show project durations in days using julianday for completed projects.
sql: SELECT ProjectName, StartDate, EndDate, CAST(julianday(EndDate) - julianday(StartDate) AS INTEGER) AS DurationDays FROM Projects WHERE EndDate IS NOT NULL ORDER BY DurationDays DESC, ProjectName;

question: List active employees who are not assigned to any project using EXCEPT.
sql: SELECT Name FROM Employees WHERE Status = 'Active' EXCEPT SELECT e.Name FROM Employees e JOIN EmployeeProjects ep ON e.EmpID = ep.EmpID ORDER BY Name;

question: Find employees who have at least one Expert skill but are not project leads.
sql: SELECT DISTINCT e.Name FROM Employees e WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM EmployeeSkills es WHERE es.EmpID = e.EmpID AND es.Proficiency = 'Expert') AND e.EmpID NOT IN (SELECT ep.EmpID FROM EmployeeProjects ep WHERE ep.Role = 'Lead') ORDER BY e.Name;

question: For each skill, return the most common proficiency level using window functions.
sql: WITH prof_counts AS (SELECT s.SkillName, es.Proficiency, COUNT(*) AS Cnt FROM Skills s JOIN EmployeeSkills es ON s.SkillID = es.SkillID GROUP BY s.SkillID, s.SkillName, es.Proficiency), ranked AS (SELECT SkillName, Proficiency, Cnt, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY SkillName ORDER BY Cnt DESC, Proficiency) AS rn FROM prof_counts) SELECT SkillName, Proficiency, Cnt FROM ranked WHERE rn = 1 ORDER BY SkillName;

question: Show department-wise lead-to-member assignment ratio.
sql: SELECT d.DeptName, SUM(CASE WHEN ep.Role = 'Lead' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS LeadAssignments, SUM(CASE WHEN ep.Role = 'Member' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS MemberAssignments, ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN ep.Role = 'Lead' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 1.0 / NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN ep.Role = 'Member' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0), 4) AS LeadMemberRatio FROM Departments d JOIN Employees e ON d.DeptID = e.DeptID JOIN EmployeeProjects ep ON e.EmpID = ep.EmpID GROUP BY d.DeptID, d.DeptName ORDER BY LeadMemberRatio DESC, d.DeptName;

question: List cities where average employee salary exceeds global average salary.
sql: SELECT l.City, AVG(e.Salary) AS CityAvgSalary FROM Employees e JOIN Departments d ON e.DeptID = d.DeptID JOIN Locations l ON d.LocationID = l.LocationID GROUP BY l.City HAVING AVG(e.Salary) > (SELECT AVG(Salary) FROM Employees) ORDER BY CityAvgSalary DESC, l.City;
